evals.prompt: How do I type a React component that accepts any native button props plus a custom variant?
evals.expected_output: Use ComponentProps<'button'> and extend it: type ButtonProps = ComponentProps<'button'> & { variant?: 'primary' | 'secondary' }. Spread {...props} to forward native props. Avoid React.FC — write function directly with typed params.

evals.prompt: How do I type a generic list component in React TypeScript?
evals.expected_output: Use a generic: type ListProps<T> = { items: T[]; render: (item: T) => ReactNode }. Define as function List<T,>({ items, render }: ListProps<T>). The comma after T avoids JSX parsing ambiguity in .tsx files.

evals.prompt: How do I type useRef for a DOM element in TypeScript?
evals.expected_output: For DOM refs: useRef<HTMLInputElement>(null) — initial value must be null for DOM elements. For mutable values: useRef<number>(0) — non-null initial. Access via ref.current, check for null before use (ref.current?.focus()).

should_not_trigger: How do I set up strict mode in tsconfig?
How do I write a discriminated union type?
How do I set up ESLint for TypeScript?
How do I implement state management with Zustand?
